# Design System: Clean Shopper
**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-31
**Source:** Generated by /design-system-generator skill from brand interview

---

## How to Use This Document
This file is referenced by CLAUDE.md and read by Claude Code at the start of every session. Apply these tokens for all UI work. Do not hardcode hex values, pixel sizes, or spacing values in components. Reference token names from tailwind.config.js instead.

---

## Brand Direction
Clean Shopper feels optimistic, natural, and unhurried — like a knowledgeable friend guiding you through a California farmers market, not a clinical database. The visual language draws from warm earth tones (cream, sand, sage, terracotta) with generous whitespace and soft shapes, balancing the calm intentionality of Headspace, the approachable utility of Instacart, and the precision and restraint of Apple. Credible without being cold.

---

## Color System

### Primary Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| primary | #6B8770 | Primary buttons, active navigation states, key CTAs. One per view. Not used for decorative elements, background fills, or secondary actions. |
| primary-light | #8FAD94 | Hover states on primary elements, selected states on chips or tags, light-mode icon fills. |
| primary-dark | #4D6654 | Pressed states on primary buttons, focus rings, high-emphasis text links. |

### Secondary Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| secondary | #E8DFD0 | Card backgrounds, surface fills, input field backgrounds. Not used for text, icons, or interactive elements. |

### Accent Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| accent | #C4714F | Highlights, badges, promotional callouts, ingredient warning indicators requiring warm emphasis. Not used for primary actions or error states. |

### Semantic Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| success | #4A7C59 | Clean product ratings, confirmed saves, positive ingredient assessments. Not used for decorative green elements. |
| warning | #C4943A | Caution-level ingredient ratings, partial match alerts, items needing attention. Not used for brand accents. |
| error | #B85450 | Error states, avoid ingredient ratings, destructive action confirmations. Not used for accent or decorative purposes. |

### Neutral Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| neutral-50 | #FDFAF5 | Page background. The base canvas for every view. |
| neutral-100 | #F5F0E8 | Card backgrounds, modal surfaces, secondary surface fills. |
| neutral-200 | #E5DDD0 | Borders, dividers, input outlines at rest. |
| neutral-400 | #B8A898 | Placeholder text, disabled input text, inactive icon fills. |
| neutral-600 | #6B5C4E | Secondary body text, supporting copy, metadata. Must meet WCAG AA on neutral-50. |
| neutral-900 | #2D2420 | Primary text, headings, high-emphasis labels. Never use pure black (#000000). |

---

## Typography

**Font Family:** DM Sans — warm, humanist, and highly legible; feels like a knowledgeable friend rather than a medical reference, pairing naturally with the earth-tone palette.
**Source:** Google Fonts

### Type Scale
| Token Name | Size | Weight | Line Height | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| display | 48px | 700 | 1.1 | Hero headlines, empty state headings, marketing moments. One per page maximum. |
| h1 | 36px | 700 | 1.2 | Page titles. One per view. |
| h2 | 28px | 600 | 1.3 | Section headers, major content groupings. |
| h3 | 22px | 600 | 1.3 | Card titles, subsection headers, modal titles. |
| h4 | 18px | 600 | 1.4 | Field group labels, small section headers, ingredient category names. |
| body | 16px | 400 | 1.6 | All body copy, descriptions, ingredient lists, product details. Default text size. |
| small | 14px | 400 | 1.5 | Supporting text, metadata, tags, secondary labels, timestamps. |
| micro | 12px | 400 | 1.4 | Legal text, fine print, source attribution, minimal UI chrome. |

---

## Spacing Scale

Base unit: 4px

| Token Name | Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| space-xs | 4px | Icon-to-label gap, tight internal chip padding, badge padding. |
| space-sm | 8px | Compact padding within small components like tags, badges, and icon buttons. |
| space-md | 16px | Standard internal padding for inputs, buttons, and list items. |
| space-lg | 24px | Card internal padding, form field spacing, content group padding. |
| space-xl | 32px | Gap between cards in a grid, space between a heading and its content. |
| space-2xl | 48px | Between major content sections on a page. |
| space-3xl | 64px | Page-level top/bottom margins, large section gaps. |
| space-4xl | 96px | Hero section padding, full-bleed section vertical spacing. |

---

## Border Radius

| Token Name | Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| radius-sm | 6px | Tags, badges, small chips, tooltips. |
| radius-md | 12px | Input fields, buttons, small cards. |
| radius-lg | 20px | Product cards, modals, drawers, larger containers. |
| radius-full | 9999px | Pills, avatar images, fully rounded icon buttons. |

---

## Shadows

| Token Name | Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| shadow-sm | 0 1px 3px rgba(45, 36, 32, 0.08) | Resting card state, subtle surface lift. Default for all cards. |
| shadow-md | 0 4px 12px rgba(45, 36, 32, 0.10) | Hover state on interactive cards, dropdown menus, popovers. |
| shadow-lg | 0 8px 24px rgba(45, 36, 32, 0.13) | Modals, overlays, focused drawer panels. |

---

## Usage Rules Summary

These rules apply to every component Claude Code builds for this project:

1. Never hardcode a hex value. Use the token name from tailwind.config.js.
2. Never hardcode a pixel font size. Use the type scale token classes.
3. Never hardcode spacing. Use the spacing scale token classes.
4. Primary color (#6B8770) is for the single most important interactive element per view. Not for decoration, background fills, or secondary actions.
5. neutral-900 for primary text. neutral-600 for secondary text. Never use pure black (#000000).
6. Semantic colors carry meaning. Do not use success green for anything other than positive ingredient or product states.
7. All text on neutral-50 or neutral-100 backgrounds must meet WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text).
8. Whitespace is a feature. Use space-xl or larger between distinct content sections. Do not compress layouts.
